# PLAMIOmini

> PLAMIOmini is a lightweight, single-game Arduino runtime for AI-assisted game development. It provides unified Graphics, Input, Audio, Storage, timing, collision, tween, and save-data APIs for RP2040, RP2350, and ESP32 targets.

PLAMIOmini is a streamlined Arduino IDE version of PLAMIO. Games derive from `PLAMIOmini::GameMini` and are combined with one concrete display, input, audio, and storage implementation in a complete Arduino `.ino` sketch. The runtime owns the approximately 30 FPS main loop; `setup()` calls `PLAMIOmini::start(...)`, and Arduino `loop()` remains empty.

For code generation, treat the supplied headers as authoritative. Never invent classes, enum values, configuration fields, constructor arguments, fonts, colors, buttons, sound presets, or function signatures. The public API in `src/PLAMIOmini.h` has the highest priority, followed by the selected driver headers and hardware configuration, the AI guidelines, and the game design document.

## Essential Documentation

- [README](README.md): Project overview, installation, supported platforms and hardware, learning path, and sample index.
- [Public API](src/PLAMIOmini.h): Authoritative game-facing API for `GameMini`, Graphics, Input, Audio, Storage, SaveData, Platform, Math, Collision, Tween, and Display constants.
- [AI Game Generation Guidelines](docs/PLAMIOmini_AI_GUIDELINES.md): Mandatory rules for generating compile-ready PLAMIOmini Arduino sketches.
- [Hardware Configuration Template](docs/PLAMIOmini_HARDWARE_CONFIG_TEMPLATE.md): Selects the board, display, input, audio, storage, pins, buses, and hardware settings used by generated sketches.
- [Game Design Template](docs/PLAMIOmini_GAME_DESIGN_TEMPLATE.md): English game-design worksheet for AI-assisted generation.
- [Game Design Template (Japanese)](docs/PLAMIOmini_GAME_DESIGN_TEMPLATE_JP.md): Japanese game-design worksheet.
- [License](LICENSE.txt): MIT License.

## Driver Headers

Read the concrete driver header selected by the hardware configuration before creating configuration objects or instances.

### Graphics

- [GraphicsILI9341](src/graphics/GraphicsILI9341.h): ILI9341 color display driver and configuration.
- [GraphicsSSD1306](src/graphics/GraphicsSSD1306.h): SSD1306 monochrome display driver and configuration.
- [GraphicsBase](src/graphics/GraphicsBase.h): Internal common graphics-driver interface.

### Input

- [GPIO Button Definitions](src/input/GpioButtons.h): GPIO button-related declarations.
- [InputGpioButtons](src/input/InputGpioButtons.h): Direct GPIO button input driver.
- [InputSnes](src/input/InputSnes.h): SNES controller input driver.
- [InputPS2](src/input/InputPS2.h): PS2 controller input driver.
- [InputBase](src/input/InputBase.h): Common button mapping and input-driver interface.

### Audio

- [AudioPWM](src/audio/AudioPWM.h): PWM tone-audio driver.
- [AudioI2S](src/audio/AudioI2S.h): I2S tone-audio driver.
- [AudioStub](src/audio/AudioStub.h): No-audio implementation.
- [AudioBase](src/audio/AudioBase.h): Common audio playback, music, sound-effect, and volume behavior.

### Storage

- [StorageSD](src/storage/StorageSD.h): SD-card storage driver.
- [StorageEEPROM](src/storage/StorageEEPROM.h): EEPROM-style storage driver.
- [StorageStub](src/storage/StorageStub.h): No-storage implementation.
- [StorageBase](src/storage/StorageBase.h): Common storage-driver interface.

## Required Game Structure

A game derives from `PLAMIOmini::GameMini` and overrides these exact functions:

```cpp
void onInit(PLAMIOmini::Storage& storage) override;
void onUpdate(PLAMIOmini::Input& input,
              PLAMIOmini::Audio& audio,
              PLAMIOmini::Storage& storage,
              float deltaSec) override;
bool onDraw(PLAMIOmini::Graphics& graphics,
            bool requestFullRedraw) override;
void onTerminate(PLAMIOmini::Storage& storage) override;
```

A complete sketch normally declares, at global scope and in this order:

1. One selected graphics configuration and instance.
2. One selected input configuration or button mapping and instance.
3. One selected storage configuration and instance.
4. One selected audio configuration and instance.
5. One `GameMini`-derived game instance.

Then call:

```cpp
void setup()
{
    PLAMIOmini::start(graphics, input, storage, audio, game);
}

void loop()
{
}
```

## Compile-Safety Rules

- Use only symbols declared exactly in the supplied headers.
- Never infer enum names from naming patterns. For example, common-looking names such as `Graphics::SIZE_12`, `Graphics::SIZE_24B`, or `Graphics::DARKBLUE` are invalid unless explicitly declared.
- Verify every `Graphics::Font`, `Graphics::Color`, `Input::Button`, `Audio::SE`, method call, configuration field, constructor, and override signature before returning generated code.
- Pass exactly one button to each input query; test combinations with separate calls.
- Do not use `HOME`, `VOL_UP`, `VOL_DOWN`, or `MUTE` for game mechanics.
- Request sound from `onUpdate()`, not `onDraw()`, and trigger it only once per event.
- Perform packaged resource-file I/O during `onInit()`, never during `onUpdate()` or `onDraw()`.
- Prefer fixed-size arrays and static constant assets. Avoid dynamic allocation and dynamic STL containers.
- Do not call `GameMini::init()`, `update()`, `draw()`, or `terminate()` directly.

## Rendering and Timing

- Use `deltaSec` for continuous movement and physics.
- Use `Platform::getMsec()` and unsigned elapsed-time arithmetic for real-time timers.
- Do not assume every frame is exactly 1/30 second.
- Fast-moving objects may use a bounded number of physics substeps.
- `onDraw()` must honor dirty rendering:

```cpp
if (!requestFullRedraw && !dirty)
{
    return false;
}

// Draw the complete frame.

dirty = false;
return true;
```

- Set `dirty = true` whenever visible state changes.
- ILI9341 visible size is 320 x 240; SSD1306 visible size is 128 x 64.
- Use only fonts declared in `Graphics::Font` and measure text when fitting or centering it.
- Important UI should preferably remain above the temporary volume OSD near the bottom of the ILI9341 screen, while backgrounds may extend to the bottom edge.

## Examples and Learning Path

- [00A AI Game Generation](examples/00A_AI_Game_Generation/00A_AI_Game_Generation.ino): Recommended AI-assisted workflow and required project files.
- [00B Hardware Setup](examples/00B_Hardware_Setup/00B_Hardware_Setup.ino): Driver includes, configuration structures, constructors, and hardware selection reference.
- [01 Hello PLAMIO](examples/01_Hello_PLAMIO/01_Hello_PLAMIO.ino): Minimal ILI9341 game structure.
- [01 Hello PLAMIO SSD1306](examples/01_Hello_PLAMIO_SSD1306/01_Hello_PLAMIO_SSD1306.ino): Minimal SSD1306 game structure.
- [02 Input Basics](examples/02_Input_Basics/02_Input_Basics.ino): Button input and movement.
- [03 Graphics Basics](examples/03_Graphics_Basics/03_Graphics_Basics.ino): Shapes, colors, fonts, and alignment.
- [04 Audio Basics](examples/04_Audio_Basics/04_Audio_Basics.ino): Sound effects and music.
- [05 Save Data](examples/05_Save_Data/05_Save_Data.ino): SaveData loading and saving.
- [06 Collision](examples/06_Collision/06_Collision.ino): Collision API demonstrations.
- [07 Animation](examples/07_Animation/07_Animation.ino): Time-based animation and Tween.
- [08 Breakout](examples/08_Breakout/08_Breakout.ino): Complete real-time action game.
- [09 Star Dodge](examples/09_Star_Dodge/09_Star_Dodge.ino): Avoidance game with visual effects.
- [10 Reversi](examples/10_Reversi/10_Reversi.ino): Board game and CPU logic.
- [11 Memory Tiles](examples/11_Memory_Tiles/11_Memory_Tiles.ino): Memory game with state transitions.

## Optional Implementation Sources

These files implement the runtime and are useful when diagnosing behavior or extending PLAMIOmini, but game generation should normally rely on the public API and selected driver headers.

- [System Runtime](src/core/SystemMini.cpp): Main loop, audio worker, volume controls, OSD, and startup behavior.
- [Graphics Implementations](src/graphics/): ILI9341, SSD1306, and shared graphics implementation.
- [Input Implementations](src/input/): GPIO, SNES, PS2, and shared input implementation.
- [Audio Implementations](src/audio/): PWM, I2S, stub, sound effects, music, and volume implementation.
- [Storage Implementations](src/storage/): SD, EEPROM, stub, and shared storage implementation.
- [Utility Implementations](src/util/): Platform timing, Math, Collision, Tween, SaveData, screenshots, and synchronization helpers.

## Package Metadata

- [Arduino Library Metadata](library.properties): Library name, version, architectures, dependency, and public include.
- [PlatformIO Configuration](platformio.ini): Development and build configuration.
- [SDK Packaging Script](scripts/package_sdk.py): Creates the AI-facing SDK and project archives.
